AWS.question: How to use the processor state control feature available on the c4.8xlarge instance?
AWS.answer:
The processor state control consists of 2 states:
The C state – Sleep state varying from c0 to c6. C6 being the
•
deepest sleep state for a processor
The P state – Performance state p0 being the highest and p15
•
being the lowest possible frequency.
Now, why the C state and P state. Processors have cores, these cores
need thermal headroom to boost their performance. Now since all the
cores are on the processor the temperature should be kept at an optimal
state so that all the cores can perform at the highest performance.
Now how will these states help in that? If a core is put into sleep state it
will reduce the overall temperature of the processor and hence other
cores can perform better. Now the same can be synchronized with other
cores, so that the processor can boost as many cores it can by timely
putting other cores to sleep, and thus get an overall performance boost.
Concluding, the C and P state can be customized in some EC2 instances
like the c4.8xlarge instance and thus you can customize the processor
according to your workload.
How to do it? You can refer this tutorial for the same.

12. To deploy a 4 node cluster of Hadoop in AWS which instance
type can be used?
First let’s understand what actually happens in a Hadoop cluster, the
Hadoop cluster follows a master slave concept. The master machine
processes all the data, slave machines store the data and act as data
nodes. Since all the storage happens at the slave, a higher capacity hard
disk would be recommended and since master does all the processing, a
higher RAM and a much better CPU is required. Therefore, you can select
the configuration of your machine depending on your workload. For e.g.
– In this case c4.8xlarge will be preferred for master machine whereas for
slave machine we can select i2.large instance. If you don’t want to deal
with configuring your instance and installing hadoop cluster manually,
you can straight away launch an Amazon EMR (Elastic Map Reduce)
instance which automatically configures the servers for you. You dump
your data to be processed in S3, EMR picks it from there, processes it,
and dumps it back into S3.

B. Make an Amazon Glacier Restore API call to load the files into
another Amazon S3 bucket within four to six hours.
C. Launch a new AWS Storage Gateway instance AMI in Amazon EC2,
and restore from a gateway snapshot.
D. Create an Amazon EBS volume from a gateway snapshot, and
mount it to an Amazon EC2 instance.
Answer C.
Explanation: The fastest way to do it would be launching a new storage
gateway instance. Why? Since time is the key factor which drives every
36 | P age
business, troubleshooting this problem will take more time. Rather than
we can just restore the previous working state of the storage gateway on
a new instance.

AWS.question: When you need to move data over long distances using the internet, for instance across countries or continents to your Amazon S3 bucket, which method or service will you use?
AWS.answer:
Amazon Glacier
B. Amazon CloudFront
C. Amazon Transfer Acceleration
D. Amazon Snowball
Answer C.
Explanation: You would not use Snowball, because for now, the
snowball service does not support cross region data transfer, and since,
we are transferring across countries, Snowball cannot be used. Transfer
Acceleration shall be the right choice here as it throttles your data
transfer with the use of optimized network paths and Amazon’s content
delivery network upto 300% compared to normal data transfer speed.

AWS.question: How can you speed up data transfer in Snowball?
AWS.answer:
The data transfer can be increased in the following way:
By performing multiple copy operations at one time i.e. if the
•
workstation is powerful enough, you can initiate multiple cp
commands each from different terminals, on the same Snowball
device.
Copying from multiple workstations to the same snowball.
•
Transferring large files or by creating a batch of small file, this will
•
reduce the encryption overhead.
Eliminating unnecessary hops i.e. make a setup where the source
•
machine(s) and the snowball are the only machines active on the
switch being used, this can hugely improve performance.

23. If you want to launch Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2)
instances and assign each instance a predetermined private IP
address you should:
Launch the instance from a private Amazon Machine Image (AMI).
B. Assign a group of sequential Elastic IP address to the instances.
C. Launch the instances in the Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
D. Launch the instances in a Placement Group.
Answer C.
Explanation: The best way of connecting to your cloud resources (for
ex- ec2 instances) from your own data center (for eg- private cloud) is a
VPC. Once you connect your datacenter to the VPC in which your
instances are present, each instance is assigned a private IP address
which can be accessed from your datacenter. Hence, you can access your
public cloud resources, as if they were on your own network.

AWS.question: Can I connect my corporate datacenter to the Amazon Cloud?
AWS.answer:
Yes, you can do this by establishing a VPN(Virtual Private Network)
connection between your company’s network and your VPC (Virtual
Private Cloud), this will allow you to interact with your EC2 instances as if
they were within your existing network.
25. Is it possible to change the private IP addresses of an EC2 while
it is running/stopped in a VPC?
Primary private IP address is attached with the instance throughout its
lifetime and cannot be changed, however secondary private addresses
can be unassigned, assigned or moved between interfaces or instances
at any point.

AWS.question: Why do you make subnets?
AWS.answer:
Because there is a shortage of networks
B. To efficiently utilize networks that have a large no. of hosts.
C. Because there is a shortage of hosts.
D. To efficiently utilize networks that have a small no. of hosts.
Answer B.
38 | P age
Explanation: If there is a network which has a large no. of hosts,
managing all these hosts can be a tedious job. Therefore we divide this
network into subnets (sub-networks) so that managing these hosts
becomes simpler.

AWS.question: Which of the following is true?
AWS.answer:
You can attach multiple route tables to a subnet
B. You can attach multiple subnets to a route table
C. Both A and B
D. None of these.
Answer B.
Explanation: Route Tables are used to route network packets, therefore
in a subnet having multiple route tables will lead to confusion as to
where the packet has to go. Therefore, there is only one route table in a
subnet, and since a route table can have any no. of records or
information, hence attaching multiple subnets to a route table is
possible.
28. In CloudFront what happens when content is NOT present at an
Edge location and a request is made to it?
An Error “404 not found” is returned
B. CloudFront delivers the content directly from the origin server and
stores it in the cache of the edge location
C. The request is kept on hold till content is delivered to the edge
location
D. The request is routed to the next closest edge location
Answer B.
Explanation: CloudFront is a content delivery system, which caches data
to the nearest edge location from the user, to reduce latency. If data is
not present at an edge location, the first time the data may get
transferred from the original server, but from the next time, it will be
served from the cached edge.
39 | P age
29. If I’m using Amazon CloudFront, can I use Direct Connect to
transfer objects from my own data center?
Yes. Amazon CloudFront supports custom origins including origins from
outside of AWS. With AWS Direct Connect, you will be charged with the
respective data transfer rates.

AWS.question: If my AWS Direct Connect fails, will I lose my connectivity?
AWS.answer:
If a backup AWS Direct connect has been configured, in the event of a
failure it will switch over to the second one. It is recommended to enable
Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) when configuring your
connections to ensure faster detection and failover. On the other hand, if
you have configured a backup IPsec VPN connection instead, all VPC
traffic will failover to the backup VPN connection automatically. Traffic
to/from public resources such as Amazon S3 will be routed over the
Internet. If you do not have a backup AWS Direct Connect link or a IPsec
VPN link, then Amazon VPC traffic will be dropped in the event of a
failure.
Section 5: Amazon Database
31. If I launch a standby RDS instance, will it be in the same
Availability Zone as my primary?
Only for Oracle RDS types
B. Yes
C. Only if it is configured at launch
D. No
Answer D.
Explanation: No, since the purpose of having a standby instance is to
avoid an infrastructure failure (if it happens), therefore the standby
instance is stored in a different availability zone, which is a physically
different independent infrastructure.

AWS.question: When would I prefer Provisioned IOPS over Standard RDS storage?
AWS.answer:
If you have batch-oriented workloads

C. If you have workloads that are not sensitive to consistent

Explanation: Provisioned IOPS deliver high IO rates but on the other

manual intervention they enable full utilization of systems, therefore
a provisioned IOPS will be preferred for batch oriented workload.

AWS.question: How is Amazon RDS, DynamoDB and Redshift different?
AWS.answer:
Amazon RDS is a database management service for relational
•
databases, it manages patching, upgrading, backing up of data
etc. of databases for you without your intervention. RDS is a Db
management service for structured data only.
DynamoDB, on the other hand, is a NoSQL database service,
•
NoSQL deals with unstructured data.
Redshift, is an entirely different service, it is a data warehouse
•
product and is used in data analysis.

Yes

instance in parallel, as the former is solely used for standby purposes, it
cannot be used unless the primary instance goes down.
35. Your company’s branch offices are all over the world, they use a

41 | P age
to all the branches. This should be done in the shortest time
possible. How will you build the DB architecture in order to meet
the requirements?
For each regional deployment, use RDS MySQL with a master in
the region and a read replica in the HQ region
B. For each regional deployment, use MySQL on EC2 with a master in

C. For each regional deployment, use RDS MySQL with a master in
the region and send hourly RDS snapshots to the HQ region
D. For each regional deployment, use MySQL on EC2 with a master in
the region and use S3 to copy data files hourly to the HQ region
Answer A.
Explanation: For this we will take an RDS instance as a master, because
it will manage our database for us and since we have to read from every
region, we’ll put a read replica of this instance in every region where the
data has to be read from. Option C is not correct since putting a read
replica would be more efficient than putting a snapshot, a read replica
can be promoted if needed to an independent DB instance, but with a
Db snapshot it becomes mandatory to launch a separate DB Instance.

AWS.question: Can I run more than one DB instance for Amazon RDS for free?
AWS.answer:
Yes. You can run more than one Single-AZ Micro database instance, that
too for free! However, any use exceeding 750 instance hours, across all
Amazon RDS Single-AZ Micro DB instances, across all eligible database
engines and regions, will be billed at standard Amazon RDS prices. For
example: if you run two Single-AZ Micro DB instances for 400 hours each
in a single month, you will accumulate 800 instance hours of usage, of
which 750 hours will be free. You will be billed for the remaining 50
hours at the standard Amazon RDS price.
For a detailed discussion on this topic, please refer our RDS AWS blog.

MapReduce, since a near real time analyses is needed.
38. Can I retrieve only a specific element of the data, if I have a

AWS.question: What happens to my backups and DB Snapshots if I delete my DB Instance?

When you delete a DB instance, you have an option of creating a final
DB snapshot, if you do that you can restore your database from that

snapshot. RDS retains this user-created DB snapshot along with all other

Snapshots are retained.

AWS.question: Which of the following use cases are suitable for Amazon DynamoDB? Choose 2 answers
AWS.answer:

You can use the COPY command to load data in parallel directly to

43. Your application has to retrieve data from your user’s mobile

Create a new Amazon DynamoDB (able each day and drop the one

Section 6: AWS Auto Scaling, AWS Load Balancer
46. Suppose you have an application where you have to render images
and also do some general computing. From the following services which
service will best fit your need?
Classic Load Balancer
46 | P age
B. Application Load Balancer
C. Both of them

the URL, therefore if your task needs image rendering it will route it to a
different instance, and for general computing it will route it to a different
instance.

AWS.question: What is the difference between Scalability and Elasticity?
AWS.answer:
Scalability is the ability of a system to increase its hardware resources to
handle the increase in demand. It can be done by increasing the
hardware specifications or increasing the processing nodes.
Elasticity is the ability of a system to handle increase in the workload by
adding additional hardware resources when the demand increases(same
as scaling) but also rolling back the scaled resources, when the resources
are no longer needed. This is particularly helpful in Cloud environments,
where a pay per use model is followed.

AWS.question: How will you change the instance type for instances which are running in your application tier and are using Auto Scaling. Where will you change it from the following areas?
AWS.answer:
Auto Scaling policy configuration
B. Auto Scaling group
C. Auto Scaling tags configuration
D. Auto Scaling launch configuration
Answer D.
Explanation: Auto scaling tags configuration, is used to attach metadata
to your instances, to change the instance type you have to use auto
scaling launch configuration.
47 | P age
49. You have a content management system running on an Amazon
EC2 instance that is approaching 100% CPU utilization. Which
option will reduce load on the Amazon EC2 instance?
Create a load balancer, and register the Amazon EC2 instance
with it
B. Create a CloudFront distribution, and configure the Amazon EC2
instance as the origin
C. Create an Auto Scaling group from the instance using the
CreateAutoScalingGroup action

CreateLaunchConfigurationAction
Answer A.
Explanation:Creating alone an autoscaling group will not solve the
issue, until you attach a load balancer to it. Once you attach a load
balancer to an autoscaling group, it will efficiently distribute the load
among all the instances. Option B – CloudFront is a CDN, it is a data
transfer tool therefore will not help reduce load on the EC2 instance.
Similarly the other option – Launch configuration is a template for
configuration which has no connection with reducing loads.

AWS.question: When should I use a Classic Load Balancer and when should I use an Application load balancer?
AWS.answer:
A Classic Load Balancer is ideal for simple load balancing of traffic across
multiple EC2 instances, while an Application Load Balancer is ideal for
microservices or container-based architectures where there is a need to
route traffic to multiple services or load balance across multiple ports on
the same EC2 instance.

AWS.question: What does Connection draining do?
AWS.answer:
Terminates instances which are not in use.
B. Re-routes traffic from instances which are to be updated or
failed a health check.
C. Re-routes traffic from instances which have more workload to
instances which have less workload.
D. Drains all the connections from an instance, with one click.
48 | P age
Answer B.
Explanation: Connection draining is a service under ELB which
constantly monitors the health of the instances. If any instance fails a
health check or if any instance has to be patched with a software update,
it pulls all the traffic from that instance and re routes them to other
instances.

AWS.question: When an instance is unhealthy, it is terminated and replaced with a new one, which of the following services does that?
AWS.answer:
Sticky Sessions
B. Fault Tolerance
C. Connection Draining
D. Monitoring
Answer B.
Explanation: When ELB detects that an instance is unhealthy, it starts
routing incoming traffic to other healthy instances in the region. If all the
instances in a region becomes unhealthy, and if you have instances in
some other availability zone/region, your traffic is directed to them. Once
your instances become healthy again, they are re routed back to the
original instances.

AWS.question: What are lifecycle hooks used for in AutoScaling?

C. They are used to shorten the wait time to a scale in or scale out
event
D. None of these
Answer B.
Explanation: Lifecycle hooks are used for putting wait time before any
lifecycle action i.e launching or terminating an instance happens. The
purpose of this wait time, can be anything from extracting log files
before terminating an instance or installing the necessary softwares in an
instance before launching it.

or other issue with your web application, and then make changes to your

AWS.question: How do I transfer my existing domain name registration to Amazon Route 53 without disrupting my existing web traffic?
AWS.answer:
You will need to get a list of the DNS record data for your domain name
first, it is generally available in the form of a “zone file” that you can get
from your existing DNS provider. Once you receive the DNS record data,
you can use Route 53’s Management Console or simple web-services
interface to create a hosted zone that will store your DNS records for
your domain name and follow its transfer process. It also includes steps
such as updating the nameservers for your domain name to the ones
associated with your hosted zone. For completing the process you have
to contact the registrar with whom you registered your domain name
and follow the transfer process. As soon as your registrar propagates the
new name server delegations, your DNS queries will start to get
answered.
Section 8: AWS SQS, AWS SNS, AWS SES, AWS ElasticBeanstalk
52 | P age

AWS.question: Which of the following services you would not use to deploy an app?
AWS.answer:
Elastic Beanstalk
B. Lambda
C. Opsworks
D. CloudFormation
Answer B.
Explanation: Lambda is used for running server-less applications. It can
be used to deploy functions triggered by events. When we say serverless,
we mean without you worrying about the computing resources running
in the background. It is not designed for creating applications which are
publicly accessed.

AWS.question: How does Elastic Beanstalk apply updates?
AWS.answer:
By having a duplicate ready with updates before swapping.
B. By updating on the instance while it is running
C. By taking the instance down in the maintenance window
D. Updates should be installed manually
Answer A.
Explanation: Elastic Beanstalk prepares a duplicate copy of the instance,
before updating the original instance, and routes your traffic to the
duplicate instance, so that, incase your updated application fails, it will
switch back to the original instance, and there will be no downtime
experienced by the users who are using your application.

AWS.question: How is AWS Elastic Beanstalk different than AWS OpsWorks?
AWS.answer:
AWS Elastic Beanstalk is an application management platform while
OpsWorks is a configuration management platform. BeanStalk is an easy
to use service which is used for deploying and scaling web applications
developed with Java, .Net, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go and Docker.
Customers upload their code and Elastic Beanstalk automatically handles
the deployment. The application will be ready to use without any
infrastructure or resource configuration.
53 | P age
In contrast, AWS Opsworks is an integrated configuration management
platform for IT administrators or DevOps engineers who want a high
degree of customization and control over operations.

AWS.question: What happens if my application stops responding to requests in beanstalk?
AWS.answer:
AWS Beanstalk applications have a system in place for avoiding failures
in the underlying infrastructure. If an Amazon EC2 instance fails for any
reason, Beanstalk will use Auto Scaling to automatically launch a new
instance. Beanstalk can also detect if your application is not responding
on the custom link, even though the infrastructure appears healthy, it will
be logged as an environmental event( e.g a bad version was deployed)
so you can take an appropriate action.
Section 9: AWS OpsWorks, AWS KMS

AWS.question: How is AWS OpsWorks different than AWS CloudFormation?
AWS.answer:
OpsWorks and CloudFormation both support application modelling,
deployment, configuration, management and related activities. Both
support a wide variety of architectural patterns, from simple web
applications to highly complex applications. AWS OpsWorks and AWS
CloudFormation differ in abstraction level and areas of focus.
AWS CloudFormation is a building block service which enables customer
to manage almost any AWS resource via JSON-based domain specific
language. It provides foundational capabilities for the full breadth of
AWS, without prescribing a particular model for development and
operations. Customers define templates and use them to provision and
manage AWS resources, operating systems and application code.
In contrast, AWS OpsWorks is a higher level service that focuses on
providing highly productive and reliable DevOps experiences for IT
administrators and ops-minded developers. To do this, AWS OpsWorks
employs a configuration management model based on concepts such as
stacks and layers, and provides integrated experiences for key activities
like deployment, monitoring, auto-scaling, and automation. Compared
to AWS CloudFormation, AWS OpsWorks supports a narrower range of
54 | P age
application-oriented AWS resource types including Amazon EC2
instances, Amazon EBS volumes, Elastic IPs, and Amazon CloudWatch
metrics.
67. I created a key in Oregon region to encrypt my data in North Virginia
region for security purposes. I added two users to the key and an
external AWS account. I wanted to encrypt an object in S3, so when I
tried, the key that I just created was not listed. What could be the
reason?
External aws accounts are not supported.
B. AWS S3 cannot be integrated KMS.
C. The Key should be in the same region.
D. New keys take some time to reflect in the list.
Answer C.
Explanation: The key created and the data to be encrypted should be in
the same region. Hence the approach taken here to secure the data is
incorrect.
68. A company needs to monitor the read and write IOPS for their
AWS MySQL RDS instance and send real-time alerts to their
operations team. Which AWS services can accomplish this?
Amazon Simple Email Service
B. Amazon CloudWatch
C. Amazon Simple Queue Service
D. Amazon Route 53
Answer B.
Explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is a cloud monitoring tool and hence
this is the right service for the mentioned use case. The other options
listed here are used for other purposes for example route 53 is used for
DNS services, therefore CloudWatch will be the apt choice.

AWS.question: What happens when one of the resources in a stack cannot be created successfully in AWS OpsWorks?
AWS.answer:
When an event like this occurs, the “automatic rollback on error” feature
is enabled, which causes all the AWS resources which were created
55 | P age
successfully till the point where the error occurred to be deleted. This is
helpful since it does not leave behind any erroneous data, it ensures the
fact that stacks are either created fully or not created at all. It is useful in
events where you may accidentally exceed your limit of the no. of Elastic
IP addresses or maybe you may not have access to an EC2 AMI that you
are trying to run etc.

AWS.question: What automation tools can you use to spinup servers?
AWS.answer:
Any of the following tools can be used:
Roll-your-own scripts, and use the AWS API tools. Such scripts
•
could be written in bash, perl or other language of your choice.
Use a configuration management and provisioning tool like puppet
•
or its successor Opscode Chef. You can also use a tool like Scalr.
Use a managed solution such as Rightscale.
•
External aws accounts are not supported.
B. AWS S3 cannot be integrated KMS.
C. The Key should be in the same region.
D. New keys take some time to reflect in the list.
Answer C.
Explanation: The key created and the data to be encrypted should be in
the same region. Hence the approach taken here to secure the data is
incorrect.
68. A company needs to monitor the read and write IOPS for their

this is the right service for the mentioned use case. The other options

AWS.question: What is AWS?
AWS.answer:
AWS (Amazon Web Services) is a cloud computing platform that provides on-demand
computing services such as compute power, storage, databases, networking, and more.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are the benefits of using AWS?
AWS.answer:
AWS offers scalability, cost efficiency, flexibility, high availability, global reach, and strong
security features.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What types of cloud computing models does AWS provide?
AWS.answer:
AWS supports Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service (PaaS), and Software
as a Service (SaaS).
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an AWS Region?
AWS.answer:
An AWS Region is a geographical area that contains multiple Availability Zones and
operates independently.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an Availability Zone (AZ)?
AWS.answer:
An Availability Zone is an isolated data center within a region designed to provide fault
tolerance and high availability.
EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is EC2 in AWS?
AWS.answer:
EC2 provides resizable compute capacity in the cloud through virtual servers called
instances.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are the different types of EC2 instances?
AWS.answer:
General Purpose, Compute Optimized, Memory Optimized, Storage Optimized, and GPU
instances.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How do you launch an EC2 instance?
AWS.answer:
Using the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, or SDK by selecting an AMI, instance type,
key pair, and security group.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an AMI?
AWS.answer:
An Amazon Machine Image is a template containing the OS, software, and configuration
required to launch an EC2 instance.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are EC2 key pairs?
AWS.answer:
Key pairs consist of a public and private key used to securely connect to EC2 instances.
S3 (Simple Storage Service)
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon S3?
AWS.answer:
Amazon S3 is an object storage service that stores data as objects in buckets with high
durability and availability.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are S3 storage classes?
AWS.answer: Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Standard-IA, One Zone-IA, Glacier, and Glacier Deep Archive.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How does versioning work in S3?
AWS.answer:
Versioning keeps multiple versions of an object to protect against accidental deletion or
overwrite.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an S3 bucket?
AWS.answer: A bucket is a container used to store objects in Amazon S3.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are S3 access control policies?
AWS.answer:
Bucket Policies and Access Control Lists (ACLs) control access to S3 buckets and objects.
RDS (Relational Database Service)
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon RDS?
AWS.answer:
RDS is a managed service for relational databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL
Server, and Aurora.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How do you ensure high availability in RDS?
AWS.answer: By enabling multi-AZ deployment, which replicates data to a standby instance.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon Aurora?
AWS.answer: Aurora is a high-performance relational database compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How do automated backups work in RDS?
AWS.answer: RDS automatically takes daily snapshots and transaction logs for point-in-time recovery.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is the difference between RDS and DynamoDB?
AWS.answer:
RDS is SQL-based and relational, while DynamoDB is a NoSQL key-value database.
Intermediate Level – EC2
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are EC2 instance types based on workload?
AWS.answer: General Purpose, Compute Optimized, Memory Optimized, and Storage Optimized.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are Spot Instances?
AWS.answer: Unused EC2 capacity offered at a lower price that can be interrupted by AWS.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Auto Scaling?
AWS.answer: Auto Scaling automatically adjusts EC2 instance count based on demand.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an Elastic IP?
AWS.answer: A static public IP address that can be associated with EC2 instances.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How can you stop and start EC2 without losing data?
AWS.answer:
By using EBS-backed instances; EBS data persists after stopping.
Intermediate Level – S3
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is S3 lifecycle management?
AWS.answer:
It automates object transition between storage classes or deletes them after a defined
time.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is S3 Cross-Region Replication?
AWS.answer: It replicates objects to another region for disaster recovery or compliance.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How can S3 objects be made public?
AWS.answer: By configuring bucket policies, ACLs, or enabling public access settings.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is S3 Transfer Acceleration?
AWS.answer: It speeds up uploads using CloudFront edge locations.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How is encryption managed in S3?
AWS.answer:
Using SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, or SSE-C encryption methods.
IAM (Identity and Access Management)
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS IAM?
AWS.answer: IAM manages access to AWS services using users, roles, and policies.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an IAM policy?
AWS.answer: A document defining permissions for AWS actions and resources.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an IAM role?
AWS.answer: An identity with permissions that can be assumed temporarily.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How is MFA implemented in AWS?
AWS.answer: By requiring a one-time code in addition to login credentials.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: IAM user vs IAM role?
AWS.answer:
Users have permanent credentials; roles provide temporary access.
AWS Lambda
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS Lambda?
AWS.answer: A serverless compute service that runs code in response to events.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How does Lambda scale?
AWS.answer: Automatically scales by handling concurrent executions.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are Lambda triggers?
AWS.answer: Events from services like S3, API Gateway, DynamoDB, and CloudWatch.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How do you monitor Lambda functions?
AWS.answer: Using CloudWatch Logs, Metrics, and AWS X-Ray.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is the maximum Lambda execution time?
AWS.answer:
15 minutes.
CloudFormation
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS CloudFormation?
AWS.answer: A service for provisioning infrastructure using code templates.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a CloudFormation stack?
AWS.answer: A collection of AWS resources managed together.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How are CloudFormation updates handled?
AWS.answer: By updating the template or parameters.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a CloudFormation template?
AWS.answer: A JSON or YAML file defining AWS resources.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is drift detection?
AWS.answer:
Identifies differences between deployed resources and templates.
DynamoDB
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon DynamoDB?
AWS.answer: A fully managed NoSQL database with high scalability.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a partition key?
AWS.answer: A key used to distribute data across partitions.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: GSI vs LSI?
AWS.answer: GSI allows queries on non-primary attributes; LSI uses alternate sort keys.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is DynamoDB auto-scaling?
AWS.answer: Automatically adjusts read/write capacity.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are DynamoDB Streams?
AWS.answer: Captures real-time data changes for event processing.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How do you ensure strong consistency?
AWS.answer: By using Consistent Read during reads.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is DynamoDB TTL?
AWS.answer:
Automatically deletes expired items.
VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an Amazon VPC?
AWS.answer: A logically isolated virtual network in AWS.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a subnet?
AWS.answer: A segment of a VPC IP range.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: Public vs private subnet?
AWS.answer: Public has internet access; private does not.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an Internet Gateway?
AWS.answer: Enables internet connectivity for VPC resources.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: NAT instance vs NAT gateway?
AWS.answer: Both allow outbound internet access for private subnets.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a route table?
AWS.answer: Defines traffic routing rules.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is VPC peering?
AWS.answer: Private connectivity between two VPCs.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a VPC endpoint?
AWS.answer: Private access to AWS services without internet.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are VPC Flow Logs?
AWS.answer: Capture IP traffic information.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: Security Groups vs NACLs?
AWS.answer:
Security Groups are instance-level; NACLs are subnet-level.
CloudWatch
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon CloudWatch?
AWS.answer: A monitoring and observability service.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are CloudWatch alarms?
AWS.answer: Triggers actions based on metrics.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a CloudWatch metric?
AWS.answer: Time-based performance data.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are CloudWatch Logs?
AWS.answer: Centralized log monitoring and analysis.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are CloudWatch Events?
AWS.answer: Triggers automated actions from system changes.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a CloudWatch dashboard?
AWS.answer: Custom visualization of metrics.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS X-Ray?
AWS.answer: Tracing service for distributed applications.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: CloudWatch vs CloudTrail?
AWS.answer:
Monitoring vs API auditing.
Elastic Beanstalk
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS Elastic Beanstalk?
AWS.answer: A PaaS for deploying and scaling applications.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How does Elastic Beanstalk simplify deployment?
AWS.answer: It manages infrastructure, scaling, and monitoring.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an environment?
AWS.answer: A version of the application with resources.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How are updates handled?
AWS.answer: Using rolling updates.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How do you monitor Beanstalk apps?
AWS.answer:
Via CloudWatch integration.
ECS & Containers
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon ECS?
AWS.answer: A container orchestration service.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: ECS vs EKS?
AWS.answer: ECS is AWS-native; EKS is Kubernetes-based.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are ECS tasks and services?
AWS.answer: Tasks run containers; services manage tasks.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an ECS cluster?
AWS.answer: A logical group of compute resources.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS Fargate?
AWS.answer:
Serverless compute for containers.
Elastic Load Balancer
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is an ELB?
AWS.answer: Distributes traffic across multiple targets.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: Types of load balancers?
AWS.answer: ALB, NLB, and Gateway Load Balancer.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: ALB vs NLB?
AWS.answer: ALB handles HTTP/HTTPS; NLB handles TCP.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a target group?
AWS.answer: Defines where traffic is routed.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How does ELB handle SSL?
AWS.answer:
Performs SSL termination.
Code Pipeline & CI/CD
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS Code Pipeline?
AWS.answer: A CI/CD service for automating releases.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: Core components of Code Pipeline?
AWS.answer: Source, build, test, and deploy stages.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How does Code Pipeline integrate with AWS?
AWS.answer: Works with Code Build, Code Deploy, Lambda, and CloudFormation.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: Code Pipeline vs Jenkins?
AWS.answer: Managed AWS service vs self-managed open-source.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS Code Build?
AWS.answer:
A managed build and test service.
Advanced Security & Identity
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS KMS?
AWS.answer: A managed encryption key service.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What encryption types does KMS support?
AWS.answer: Symmetric and asymmetric.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS Secrets Manager?
AWS.answer: Secure storage for credentials and secrets.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS Shield?
AWS.answer: DDoS protection service.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon GuardDuty?
AWS.answer: Threat detection and monitoring service.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS WAF?
AWS.answer: Web application firewall.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is Amazon Inspector?
AWS.answer: Automated vulnerability assessment.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What are AWS Organizations policies?
AWS.answer: Centralized account management and governance.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: How does IAM role delegation work?
AWS.answer: Provides temporary access without sharing credentials.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is a service-linked role?
AWS.answer: A role automatically created for AWS services to access resources.
AWS.source: AWS Interview Questions_ Beginner to Advanced Guide.pdf

AWS.question: What is AWS?
AWS.answer:
AWS (Amazon Web Services) is a cloud computing platform that o�ers on-
demand computing resources and services such as storage, databases,
networking, and more.

AWS.question: What are the benefits of using AWS?
AWS.answer:
Benefits include scalability, cost e�ciency, flexibility, security, and high
availability.

AWS.question: What types of cloud computing models does AWS o�er?
AWS.answer:
The models include Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service
(PaaS), and Software as a Service (SaaS).

AWS.question: What is an AWS Region?
AWS.answer:
A region is a specific geographical area that contains multiple AWS data centers
(availability zones). Each region operates independently and provides services
closer to users for reduced latency.

AWS.question: What is an Availability Zone?
AWS.answer:
An Availability Zone (AZ) is a physical data center located within an AWS Region.
Each region has multiple AZs to ensure fault tolerance.
EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)

AWS.question: What is EC2 in AWS?
AWS.answer:
EC2 is a web service that o�ers resizable compute capacity in the cloud,
enabling users to launch and manage virtual machines (instances).

AWS.question: What are the di�erent types of EC2 instances?
AWS.answer:
Instance types include General Purpose (e.g., t2, t3), Compute Optimized (e.g.,
c5), Memory Optimized (e.g., r5), Storage Optimized (e.g., i3), and GPU Instances
(e.g., p3).

AWS.question: How do you launch an EC2 instance?
AWS.answer:
You can launch an instance through the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, or
AWS SDKs by selecting an AMI, instance type, and security settings.

AWS.question: What is an AMI (Amazon Machine Image)?
AWS.answer:
An AMI is a pre-configured template that contains the operating system,
application server, and applications necessary to launch an EC2 instance.

AWS.question: What are EC2 key pairs?
AWS.answer:
Key pairs are used for secure login to EC2 instances, consisting of a public key
(stored in AWS) and a private key (kept by the user).
S3 (Simple Storage Service)

AWS.question: What is Amazon S3?
AWS.answer:
Amazon S3 is an object storage service that saves data as objects within
buckets, providing high availability, scalability, and durability.

AWS.question: What are the storage classes in S3?
AWS.answer:
The storage classes include Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Standard-IA, One
Zone-IA, Glacier, and Glacier Deep Archive.

AWS.question: How does versioning work in S3?
AWS.answer:
S3 versioning allows multiple versions of an object to be stored in a bucket,
protecting against accidental overwrites or deletions.

AWS.question: What is an S3 bucket?
AWS.answer:
A bucket is a container used to store objects (files) in Amazon S3, with each
bucket having a unique name.

AWS.question: What are S3 access control policies?
AWS.answer:
S3 uses Bucket Policies and ACLs (Access Control Lists) to manage access to
buckets and objects.
RDS (Relational Database Service)

AWS.question: What is Amazon RDS?
AWS.answer:
RDS is a managed database service that supports relational databases like
MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and Amazon Aurora.

AWS.question: How do you ensure high availability in RDS?
AWS.answer:
RDS Multi-AZ deployment enhances availability by automatically replicating
data to a standby instance in another AZ.

AWS.question: What is Amazon Aurora?
AWS.answer:
Amazon Aurora is a high-performance relational database engine compatible
with MySQL and PostgreSQL, designed for cloud scalability and availability.

AWS.question: How does automated backup work in RDS?
AWS.answer:
RDS conducts daily automatic backups and retains transaction logs, facilitating
point-in-time recovery.

AWS.question: What is the di�erence between RDS and DynamoDB?
AWS.answer:
RDS is for relational databases (SQL-based), while DynamoDB is a NoSQL
database service designed for high throughput.
Intermediate-Level Questions
EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)

AWS.question: What are the EC2 instance types based on workload?
AWS.answer:
Instance types include General Purpose (e.g., t3, m5), Compute Optimized (e.g.,
c5), Memory Optimized (e.g., r5), and Storage Optimized (e.g., i3).

AWS.question: What are Spot Instances?
AWS.answer:
Spot Instances are unused EC2 instances available at discounted rates, which
can be interrupted by AWS with little notice.

AWS.question: What is Auto Scaling in AWS EC2?
AWS.answer:
Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the number of EC2 instances based on
demand to sustain application performance and cost e�ciency.

AWS.question: What is an Elastic IP in AWS?
AWS.answer:
An Elastic IP is a static, public IP address that can be linked to any instance or
network interface in a VPC.

AWS.question: How do you stop and start an EC2 instance without losing data?
AWS.answer:
Stopping an instance shuts it down while preserving the attached EBS volumes.
However, data on the instance store (ephemeral storage) will be lost.
S3 (Simple Storage Service)

AWS.question: What is S3 lifecycle management?
AWS.answer:
Lifecycle management automates the transition of objects between storage
classes or deletes them after a defined period.

AWS.question: What is S3 Cross-Region Replication (CRR)?
AWS.answer:
CRR automatically replicates objects in an S3 bucket to another bucket in a
di�erent AWS Region for disaster recovery or low-latency access.

AWS.question: How can you make S3 objects publicly accessible?
AWS.answer:
By configuring S3 bucket policies, ACLs, or enabling public read permissions for
the objects.

AWS.question: What is S3 Transfer Acceleration?
AWS.answer:
This feature accelerates file uploads to S3 by utilizing Amazon CloudFront's
globally distributed edge locations.

AWS.question: How is encryption managed in S3?
AWS.answer:
S3 supports server-side encryption (SSE) using S3-managed keys (SSE-S3),
KMS-managed keys (SSE-KMS), and customer-provided keys (SSE-C).
IAM (Identity and Access Management)

AWS.question: What is AWS IAM?
AWS.answer:
IAM is a service that enables users to securely control access to AWS resources
through policies, roles, and user accounts.

AWS.question: What is an IAM policy?
AWS.answer:
An IAM policy specifies permissions for actions on AWS resources and is
attached to users, groups, or roles to manage access.

AWS.question: What is an IAM role?
AWS.answer:
A role is an AWS identity with permission policies that can be assumed by
entities like users, applications, or services.

AWS.question: How do you implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) in AWS?
AWS.answer:
MFA adds an extra security layer by requiring users to input a one-time
password in addition to their regular credentials.

AWS.question: What distinguishes an IAM user from a role?
AWS.answer:
An IAM user is an AWS identity linked to login credentials, while a role is
designed for granting temporary access to AWS resources.
Advanced-Level Questions
Lambda (Serverless Computing)

AWS.question: What is AWS Lambda?
AWS.answer:
AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that executes code in response to
events without the need to provision or manage servers.

AWS.question: How does Lambda scale?
AWS.answer:
Lambda scales automatically by running code in response to triggers, capable
of processing thousands of concurrent executions.

AWS.question: What are Lambda triggers?
AWS.answer:
Triggers are events that invoke Lambda functions, such as S3 uploads, API
Gateway requests, DynamoDB table updates, or scheduled CloudWatch events.

AWS.question: How do you monitor Lambda functions?
AWS.answer:
Monitoring is done using AWS CloudWatch Logs for logging, CloudWatch
Metrics for performance oversight, and AWS X-Ray for tracing.

AWS.question: What is the maximum execution time for a Lambda function?
AWS.answer:
The maximum execution time allowed for a Lambda function is 15 minutes.
CloudFormation (Infrastructure as Code)

AWS.question: What is AWS CloudFormation?
AWS.answer:
CloudFormation is a service that enables you to model and set up your AWS
resources through templates, facilitating Infrastructure as Code (IaC).

AWS.question: What is a CloudFormation stack?
AWS.answer:
A stack refers to a collection of AWS resources managed as a single unit using a
CloudFormation template.

AWS.question: How do you handle updates to a CloudFormation stack?
AWS.answer:
You can update a stack by altering its template or parameters, and
CloudFormation will implement the changes systematically and in a controlled
manner.

AWS.question: What is a CloudFormation template?
AWS.answer:
A template is a file in JSON or YAML format that specifies the AWS resources
and their configurations.

AWS.question: What is CloudFormation drift detection?
AWS.answer:
Drift detection identifies any discrepancies between the resources in your
stack and the expected configuration as defined in your template.
DynamoDB (NoSQL Database)

AWS.question: What is Amazon DynamoDB?
AWS.answer:
DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service that delivers fast and
reliable performance, along with e�ortless scalability.

AWS.question: What is a DynamoDB partition key?
AWS.answer:
The partition key, also known as the hash key, is a unique attribute that
DynamoDB utilizes to distribute data across multiple partitions for storage.

AWS.question: What are DynamoDB global and local secondary indexes?
AWS.answer:
A Global Secondary Index (GSI) enables querying based on attributes other
than the primary key across the entire table. In contrast, a Local Secondary
Index (LSI) allows querying with a di�erent sort key within the same partition as
the primary key.

AWS.question: What is DynamoDB auto-scaling?
AWS.answer:
Auto-scaling automatically adjusts the read and write capacity of your
DynamoDB table in response to tra�c, preventing both under- and over-
provisioning of resources.

AWS.question: What is DynamoDB Streams?
AWS.answer:
DynamoDB Streams captures table activities (insert, update, delete) and
facilitates real-time processing of this data, typically in conjunction with
Lambda.

AWS.question: How do you ensure strong consistency in DynamoDB?
AWS.answer:
By default, DynamoDB operates with eventual consistency. To obtain strong
consistency, you must specify "ConsistentRead" during a read operation.

AWS.question: What is DynamoDB TTL (Time to Live)?
AWS.answer:
TTL automatically deletes expired data from your DynamoDB tables, assisting
in the management of storage costs.
VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)

AWS.question: What is an Amazon VPC?
AWS.answer:
Amazon VPC allows you to create a logically isolated area of the AWS cloud to
launch AWS resources within a specified virtual network.

AWS.question: What is a subnet in VPC?
AWS.answer:
A subnet is a portion of a VPC's IP address space where you can deploy EC2
instances, categorized as either public or private.

AWS.question: What is the di�erence between a public and a private subnet?
AWS.answer:
Public subnets have a route to an internet gateway, allowing external access,
whereas private subnets lack direct internet connectivity.

AWS.question: What is an Internet Gateway?
AWS.answer:
An Internet Gateway facilitates communication between instances in your VPC
and the internet.

AWS.question: What are NAT instances and NAT gateways?
AWS.answer:
NAT instances and NAT gateways enable instances in a private subnet to access
the internet while preventing inbound tra�c.

AWS.question: What is a route table in VPC?
AWS.answer:
A route table consists of rules that dictate where network tra�c from your
subnets is directed.

AWS.question: What is VPC peering?
AWS.answer:
VPC peering allows for a private connection between two VPCs, enabling them
to communicate as if they were on the same network.

AWS.question: What is a VPC endpoint?
AWS.answer:
A VPC endpoint provides a private connection from your VPC to supported
AWS services without the need for an internet gateway or NAT.

AWS.question: What are VPC flow logs?
AWS.answer:
VPC Flow Logs o�er detailed insights into IP tra�c to and from network
interfaces in your VPC, which is helpful for security and troubleshooting
purposes.

AWS.question: What are security groups and NACLs?
AWS.answer:
Security Groups function as virtual firewalls for your instances, managing both
inbound and outbound tra�c, while Network ACLs (NACLs) regulate tra�c at
the subnet level.
CloudWatch (Monitoring)

AWS.question: What is Amazon CloudWatch?
AWS.answer:
CloudWatch is a monitoring and observability service that provides insights and
data on AWS resources and applications by collecting metrics and logs.

AWS.question: What are CloudWatch Alarms?
AWS.answer:
Alarms monitor metrics and trigger notifications or automated actions when a
specified threshold is exceeded.

AWS.question: What is a CloudWatch Metric?
AWS.answer:
Metrics are sets of time-ordered data points used for monitoring resources and
applications.

AWS.question: What is CloudWatch Logs?
AWS.answer:
CloudWatch Logs allow for real-time monitoring of logs from AWS services,
applications, and systems, enabling searching and filtering of log data.

AWS.question: What is CloudWatch Events?
AWS.answer:
CloudWatch Events provide a stream of system events that describe changes in
AWS resources, allowing for automated responses or the invocation of Lambda
functions.

AWS.question: How do you set up a CloudWatch dashboard?
AWS.answer:
A CloudWatch dashboard is a customizable interface that lets you visualize
metrics from various AWS services in one place.

AWS.question: What is AWS X-Ray?
AWS.answer:
X-Ray is a service that helps debug and analyze distributed applications by
providing end-to-end tracing of requests as they move through your services.

AWS.question: What distinguishes CloudWatch from CloudTrail?
AWS.answer:
CloudWatch focuses on performance monitoring (metrics, logs), while
CloudTrail is centered on auditing API calls made within your AWS account.
Elastic Beanstalk (PaaS)

AWS.question: What is AWS Elastic Beanstalk?
AWS.answer:
Elastic Beanstalk is a Platform as a Service (PaaS) that simplifies the
deployment and scaling of web applications and services built in popular
programming languages.

AWS.question: How does Elastic Beanstalk simplify application deployment?
AWS.answer:
Elastic Beanstalk handles infrastructure provisioning, load balancing, scaling,
and monitoring, allowing developers to focus on coding.

AWS.question: What is an environment in Elastic Beanstalk?
AWS.answer:
An environment is a collection of AWS resources running a specific version of
an application within Elastic Beanstalk.

AWS.question: How does Elastic Beanstalk manage updates?
AWS.answer:
Elastic Beanstalk employs rolling updates to avoid downtime, deploying new
instances while phasing out old ones.

AWS.question: How do you monitor applications in Elastic Beanstalk?
AWS.answer:
Elastic Beanstalk integrates with CloudWatch to monitor resource usage,
application logs, and metrics.
ECS (Elastic Container Service)

AWS.question: What is Amazon ECS?
AWS.answer:
ECS is a fully managed container orchestration service that allows you to run
Docker containers on a cluster of EC2 instances.

AWS.question: What is the di�erence between ECS and EKS?
AWS.answer:
ECS is an AWS-native service for container management, while EKS is AWS's
managed Kubernetes service for container orchestration.

AWS.question: What are ECS tasks and services?
AWS.answer:
A task is a single instance of a running container, and an ECS service manages
the number of task instances and load balancing.

AWS.question: What is an ECS cluster?
AWS.answer:
A cluster is a logical grouping of EC2 instances or Fargate tasks where ECS tasks
are initiated.

AWS.question: What is Amazon Fargate?
AWS.answer:
Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers, allowing you to run
containers without managing the underlying EC2 instances.
Elastic Load Balancer (ELB)

AWS.question: What is an Elastic Load Balancer (ELB)?
AWS.answer:
ELB automatically distributes incoming tra�c across multiple EC2 instances,
containers, or IP addresses to enhance availability.

AWS.question: What types of load balancers exist in AWS?
AWS.answer:
There are three types: Application Load Balancer (ALB), Network Load Balancer
(NLB), and Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB).

AWS.question: What distinguishes an ALB from an NLB?
AWS.answer:
ALB operates at the application layer (HTTP/HTTPS), while NLB functions at the
transport layer (TCP), providing low-latency load balancing.

AWS.question: What is a target group in ELB?
AWS.answer:
A target group directs requests to one or more registered targets, such as EC2
instances, containers, or IP addresses.

AWS.question: How does ELB manage SSL termination?
AWS.answer:
ELB can handle SSL certificates and perform SSL termination at the load
balancer level, relieving backend servers of SSL processing.
CodePipeline (CI/CD)

AWS.question: What is AWS CodePipeline?
AWS.answer:
CodePipeline is a continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD)
service that automates the release pipeline for application updates.

AWS.question: What are the primary components of CodePipeline?
AWS.answer:
Key components include sources (where code is stored), build providers (like
CodeBuild), test stages, and deployment stages.

AWS.question: How does CodePipeline integrate with other AWS services?
AWS.answer:
CodePipeline collaborates with services such as CodeBuild, CodeDeploy,
Lambda, S3, and CloudFormation to create a comprehensive CI/CD pipeline.

AWS.question: What is the di�erence between CodePipeline and Jenkins?
AWS.answer:
CodePipeline is a fully managed AWS service for CI/CD, whereas Jenkins is an
open-source CI tool that requires more manual setup and maintenance.

AWS.question: What is AWS CodeBuild?
AWS.answer:
CodeBuild is a fully managed build service that compiles source code, runs
tests, and generates software packages for deployment.
Advanced Security & Identity Management

AWS.question: What is AWS KMS (Key Management Service)?
AWS.answer:
KMS is a managed service for creating and controlling encryption keys used to
encrypt and decrypt data across AWS services.

AWS.question: What encryption types does AWS KMS support?
AWS.answer: It supports both symmetric and asymmetric encryption.

AWS.question: What is AWS Secrets Manager?
AWS.answer:
Secrets Manager provides secure storage, retrieval, and management of
sensitive information such as database credentials or API keys.

AWS.question: What is AWS Shield?
AWS.answer:
AWS Shield is a managed DDoS protection service that defends applications
running on AWS from network and application layer attacks.

AWS.question: What is Amazon GuardDuty?
AWS.answer:
GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors AWS
accounts and workloads for malicious or unauthorized activities.

AWS.question: What is AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)?
AWS.answer:
AWS WAF is a web application firewall designed to safeguard web applications
from common attacks such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting.

AWS.question: What is Amazon Inspector?
AWS.answer:
Amazon Inspector automatically evaluates applications for vulnerabilities and
deviations from best practices.

AWS.question: What is an AWS Organizations policy?
AWS.answer:
Organizations policies enable centralized management of security, auditing, and
compliance for multiple AWS accounts within your organization.

AWS.question: How does AWS IAM role delegation operate?
AWS.answer:
Role delegation provides temporary access to AWS resources without the need
to share long-term credentials by assuming IAM roles.

AWS.question: What is a service-linked role in AWS IAM?
AWS.answer:
A service-linked role is automatically generated by AWS services to enable
them to access resources on your behalf.

AWS.question: What is AWS?

Benefits include scalability, cost e�ciency, flexibility, security, and high

AWS.answer:
The models include Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service

AWS.question: What is an AWS Region?
AWS.answer:
A region is a specific geographical area that contains multiple AWS data centers
(availability zones). Each region operates independently and provides services
closer to users for reduced latency.

AWS.question: What is an Availability Zone?
AWS.answer:
An Availability Zone (AZ) is a physical data center located within an AWS Region.
Each region has multiple AZs to ensure fault tolerance.
EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)

AWS.question: What is EC2 in AWS?
AWS.answer:
EC2 is a web service that o�ers resizable compute capacity in the cloud,
enabling users to launch and manage virtual machines (instances).

AWS.question: What are the di�erent types of EC2 instances?
AWS.answer:
Instance types include General Purpose (e.g., t2, t3), Compute Optimized (e.g.,
c5), Memory Optimized (e.g., r5), Storage Optimized (e.g., i3), and GPU Instances
(e.g., p3).

AWS.question: How do you launch an EC2 instance?
AWS.answer:
You can launch an instance through the AWS Management Console, AWS CLI, or
AWS SDKs by selecting an AMI, instance type, and security settings.

AWS.question: What is an AMI (Amazon Machine Image)?
AWS.answer:
An AMI is a pre-configured template that contains the operating system,
application server, and applications necessary to launch an EC2 instance.

AWS.question: What are EC2 key pairs?
AWS.answer:
Key pairs are used for secure login to EC2 instances, consisting of a public key
(stored in AWS) and a private key (kept by the user).
S3 (Simple Storage Service)

AWS.question: What is Amazon S3?
AWS.answer:
Amazon S3 is an object storage service that saves data as objects within
buckets, providing high availability, scalability, and durability.

AWS.question: What are the storage classes in S3?
AWS.answer:
The storage classes include Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Standard-IA, One
Zone-IA, Glacier, and Glacier Deep Archive.

AWS.question: How does versioning work in S3?
AWS.answer:
S3 versioning allows multiple versions of an object to be stored in a bucket,
protecting against accidental overwrites or deletions.

AWS.question: What is an S3 bucket?
AWS.answer:
A bucket is a container used to store objects (files) in Amazon S3, with each
bucket having a unique name.

AWS.question: What are S3 access control policies?
AWS.answer:
S3 uses Bucket Policies and ACLs (Access Control Lists) to manage access to
buckets and objects.
RDS (Relational Database Service)

AWS.question: What is Amazon RDS?
AWS.answer:
RDS is a managed database service that supports relational databases like
MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and Amazon Aurora.

AWS.question: How do you ensure high availability in RDS?
AWS.answer:
RDS Multi-AZ deployment enhances availability by automatically replicating
data to a standby instance in another AZ.

AWS.question: What is Amazon Aurora?
AWS.answer:
Amazon Aurora is a high-performance relational database engine compatible
with MySQL and PostgreSQL, designed for cloud scalability and availability.

AWS.question: How does automated backup work in RDS?
AWS.answer:
RDS conducts daily automatic backups and retains transaction logs, facilitating
point-in-time recovery.

AWS.question: What is the di�erence between RDS and DynamoDB?
AWS.answer:
RDS is for relational databases (SQL-based), while DynamoDB is a NoSQL
database service designed for high throughput.

AWS.answer:
Spot Instances are unused EC2 instances available at discounted rates, which

AWS.answer:

AWS.answer:

AWS.answer:
Stopping an instance shuts it down while preserving the attached EBS volumes.

AWS.answer:
Lifecycle management automates the transition of objects between storage
classes or deletes them after a defined period.

AWS.answer:

AWS.answer:

AWS.answer:

AWS.question: How is encryption managed in S3?
AWS.answer:

AWS.answer:
An IAM policy specifies permissions for actions on AWS resources and is

AWS.question: What is an IAM role?
AWS.answer:
A role is an AWS identity with permission policies that can be assumed by
entities like users, applications, or services.

AWS.question: How do you implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) in AWS?
AWS.answer:
MFA adds an extra security layer by requiring users to input a one-time

AWS.question: What distinguishes an IAM user from a role?
AWS.answer:

A target group directs requests to one or more registered targets, such as EC2
instances, containers, or IP addresses.

AWS.question: How does ELB manage SSL termination?
AWS.answer:
ELB can handle SSL certificates and perform SSL termination at the load
balancer level, relieving backend servers of SSL processing.
CodePipeline (CI/CD)

AWS.question: What is AWS CodePipeline?
AWS.answer:
CodePipeline is a continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD)
service that automates the release pipeline for application updates.

AWS.question: What are the primary components of CodePipeline?
AWS.answer:
Key components include sources (where code is stored), build providers (like
CodeBuild), test stages, and deployment stages.

AWS.question: How does CodePipeline integrate with other AWS services?
AWS.answer:
CodePipeline collaborates with services such as CodeBuild, CodeDeploy,
Lambda, S3, and CloudFormation to create a comprehensive CI/CD pipeline.

AWS.question: What is the di�erence between CodePipeline and Jenkins?
AWS.answer:
CodePipeline is a fully managed AWS service for CI/CD, whereas Jenkins is an
open-source CI tool that requires more manual setup and maintenance.

AWS.question: What is AWS CodeBuild?
AWS.answer:
CodeBuild is a fully managed build service that compiles source code, runs
tests, and generates software packages for deployment.
Advanced Security & Identity Management

AWS.question: What is AWS KMS (Key Management Service)?
AWS.answer:
KMS is a managed service for creating and controlling encryption keys used to
encrypt and decrypt data across AWS services.

AWS.question: What encryption types does AWS KMS support?
AWS.answer: It supports both symmetric and asymmetric encryption.

AWS.question: What is AWS Secrets Manager?
AWS.answer:
Secrets Manager provides secure storage, retrieval, and management of
sensitive information such as database credentials or API keys.

AWS.question: What is AWS Shield?
AWS.answer:
AWS Shield is a managed DDoS protection service that defends applications
running on AWS from network and application layer attacks.

AWS.question: What is Amazon GuardDuty?
AWS.answer:
GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors AWS
accounts and workloads for malicious or unauthorized activities.

AWS.question: What is AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)?
AWS.answer:
AWS WAF is a web application firewall designed to safeguard web applications
from common attacks such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting.

AWS.question: What is Amazon Inspector?
AWS.answer:
Amazon Inspector automatically evaluates applications for vulnerabilities and
deviations from best practices.

AWS.question: What is an AWS Organizations policy?
AWS.answer:
Organizations policies enable centralized management of security, auditing, and
compliance for multiple AWS accounts within your organization.

AWS.question: How does AWS IAM role delegation operate?
AWS.answer:
Role delegation provides temporary access to AWS resources without the need
to share long-term credentials by assuming IAM roles.

AWS.question: What is a service-linked role in AWS IAM?
AWS.answer:
A service-linked role is automatically generated by AWS services to enable
them to access resources on your behalf.

AWS.question: What is AWS?
AWS.answer:
AWS stands for Amazon Web Services. AWS is a platform that provides on-demand
resources for hosting web services, storage, networking, databases and other resources over the
internet with a pay-as-you-go pricing.

AWS.question: What are the components of AWS?
AWS.answer:
EC2 – Elastic Compute Cloud, S3 – Simple Storage Service, Route53, EBS – Elastic Block
Store, Cloudwatch, Key-Paris are few of the components of AWS.

AWS.question: What are key-pairs?
AWS.answer:
Key-pairs are secure login information for your instances/virtual machines. To connect to the
instances we use key-pairs that contain a public-key and private-key.

AWS.question: What is S3?
AWS.answer:
S3 stands for Simple Storage Service. It is a storage service that provides an interface that
you can use to store any amount of data, at any time, from anywhere in the world. With S3 you pay
only for what you use and the payment model is pay-as-you-go.

AWS.question: What are the pricing models for EC2instances?
AWS.answer:
The different pricing model for EC2 instances are as below,
• On-demand
• Reserved
• Spot
• Scheduled
• Dedicated

AWS.question: What are the types of volumes for EC2 instances?
AWS.answer:
• There are two types of volumes,
• Instance store volumes
• EBS – Elastic Block Stores

AWS.question: What are EBS volumes?
AWS.answer:
EBS stands for Elastic Block Stores. They are persistent volumes that you can attach to the
instances. With EBS volumes, your data will be preserved even when you stop your instances, unlike
your instance store volumes where the data is deleted when you stop the instances.

AWS.question: What are the types of volumes in EBS?
AWS.answer:
Following are the types of volumes in EBS,
• General purpose
• Provisioned IOPS
• Magnetic
• Cold HDD
• Throughput optimized

AWS.question: What are the different types of instances?
AWS.answer:
Following are the types of instances,
• General purpose
• Computer Optimized
• Storage Optimized
• Memory Optimized
• Accelerated Computing

AWS.question: What is an auto-scaling and what are the components?
AWS.answer:
Auto scaling allows you to automatically scale-up and scale-down the number of instances
depending on the CPU utilization or memory utilization. There are 2 components in Auto scaling, they
are Auto-scaling groups and Launch Configuration.

AWS.question: What are reserved instances?
AWS.answer:
Reserved instances are the instance that you can reserve a fixed capacity of EC2 instances. In
reserved instances you will have to get into a contract of 1 year or 3 years.

AWS.question: What is an AMI?
AWS.answer:
AMI stands for Amazon Machine Image. AMI is a template that contains the software
configurations, launch permission and a block device mapping that specifies the volume to attach to
the instance when it is launched.

AWS.question: What is an EIP?
AWS.answer:
EIP stands for Elastic IP address. It is designed for dynamic cloud computing. When you
want to have a static IP address for your instances when you stop and restart your instances, you will
be using EIP address.

AWS.question: What is Cloudwatch?
AWS.answer:
Cloudwatch is a monitoring tool that you can use to monitor your various AWS resources.
Like health check, network, Application, etc.

AWS.question: What are the types in cloudwatch?
AWS.answer:
There are 2 types in cloudwatch. Basic monitoring and detailed monitoring. Basic monitoring
is free and detailed monitoring is chargeable.

AWS.question: What are the cloudwatch metrics that are available for EC2 instances?
AWS.answer:
Diskreads, Diskwrites, CPU utilization, networkpacketsIn, networkpacketsOut, networkIn,
networkOut, CPUCreditUsage, CPUCreditBalance.

AWS.question: What is the minimum and maximum size of individual objects that you can store in S3
AWS.answer:
The minimum size of individual objects that you can store in S3 is 0 bytes and the maximum
bytes that you can store for individual objects is 5TB.

AWS.question: What are the different storage classes in S3?
AWS.answer:
Following are the types of storage classes in S3,
• Standard frequently accessed
• Standard infrequently accessed • One-zone infrequently accessed.
• Glacier
• RRS – reduced redundancy storage

AWS.question: What is the default storage class in S3?
AWS.answer: The default storage class in S3 in Standard frequently accessed.

AWS.question: What is glacier?
AWS.answer: Glacier is the back up or archival tool that you use to back up your data in S3.

AWS.question: How can you secure the access to your S3 bucket?
AWS.answer:
There are two ways that you can control the access to your S3 buckets,
• ACL – Access Control List
• Bucket polices

AWS.question: How can you encrypt data in S3?
AWS.answer:
You can encrypt the data by using the below methods,
• Server Side Encryption – S3 (AES 256 encryption)
• Server Side Encryption – KMS (Key management Service)
• Server Side Encryption – C (Client Side)

AWS.question: What are the parameters for S3 pricing?
AWS.answer:
The pricing model for S3 is as below,
• Storage used
• Number of requests you make
• Storage management
• Data transfer
• Transfer acceleration

AWS.question: What is the pre-requisite to work with Cross region replication in S3?
AWS.answer:
You need to enable versioning on both source bucket and destination to work with cross
region replication. Also both the source and destination bucket should be in different region.

AWS.question: What are roles?
AWS.answer:
Roles are used to provide permissions to entities that you trust within your AWS account.
Roles are users in another account. Roles are similar to users but with roles you do not need to create
any username and password to work with the resources.

AWS.question: What are policies and what are the types of policies?
AWS.answer:
Policies are permissions that you can attach to the users that you create. These policies will
contain that access that you have provided to the users that you have created. There are 2 types of
policies.
• Managed policies
• Inline policies

AWS.question: What is cloudfront?
AWS.answer:
Cloudfront is an AWS web service that provided businesses and application developers an
easy and efficient way to distribute their content with low latency and high data transfer speeds.
Cloudfront is content delivery network of AWS.

AWS.question: What are edge locations?
AWS.answer:
Edge location is the place where the contents will be cached. When a user tries to access
some content, the content will be searched in the edge location. If it is not available then the content
will be made available from the origin location and a copy will be stored in the edge location.

AWS.question: What is the maximum individual archive that you can store in glacier?
AWS.answer: You can store a maximum individual archive of upto 40 TB.

AWS.question: What is VPC?
AWS.answer:
VPC stands for Virtual Private Cloud. VPC allows you to easily customize your networking
configuration. VPC is a network that is logically isolated from other network in the cloud. It allows
you to have your own IP address range, subnets, internet gateways, NAT gateways and security
groups.

AWS.question: What is VPC peering connection?
AWS.answer:
VPC peering connection allows you to connect 1 VPC with another VPC. Instances in these
VPC behave as if they are in the same network.

AWS.question: What are NAT gateways?
AWS.answer:
NAT stands for Network Address Translation. NAT gateways enables instances in a private
subnet to connect to the internet but prevent the internet from initiating a connection with those
instances.

AWS.question: How can you control the security to your VPC?
AWS.answer:
You can use security groups and NACL (Network Access Control List) to control the
security to your
VPC.

AWS.question: What are the different types of storage gateway?
AWS.answer:
Following are the types of storage gateway.
• File gateway
• Volume gateway
• Tape gateway

AWS.question: What is a snowball?
AWS.answer:
Snowball is a data transport solution that used source appliances to transfer large amounts of
data into and out of AWS. Using snowball, you can move huge amount of data from one place to
another which reduces your network costs, long transfer times and also provides better security.

AWS.question: What are the database types in RDS?
